input: Model scenarios for: Your lead asset (PD-1 inhibitor + novel ADC combination) misses its Phase 3 primary endpoint (PFS) in first-line NSCLC by a narrow margin (HR 0.82, p=0.06). What are the options?

key_factors: HR trend is positive but not significant
OS data immature — may mature favorably
subgroup analysis may show benefit in biomarker-defined population

input: Model scenarios for a pharma company whose top revenue product ($8B/year) loses patent exclusivity in 18 months. What strategic options exist?
Model scenarios: Your approved drug (JAK inhibitor for RA) just received a new boxed warning for MACE events based on a post-marketing safety study. Model the impact and response options.

Model go/no-go decision: Novel allosteric KRAS inhibitor with IC50 30 nM, moderate oral bioavailability (35%), and 60% TGI in PDX model. IND-enabling studies will cost $5M. Competitor (MRTX1133) is 1 year ahead in Phase 1.

branches_required: proceed with de-risking studies first (conditional go)
key_factors: opportunity cost vs other pipeline assets

input: Model scenarios: Should a biotech invest $50M in building an mRNA manufacturing platform for oncology, given that mRNA vaccines succeeded for COVID but oncology mRNA is unproven?

branches_required: pivot platform to other therapeutic areas

input: Model scenarios for FDA submission strategy: You have positive Phase 2 data (ORR 38%, N=80) for a novel agent in a rare sarcoma. Options: seek accelerated approval now vs wait for Phase 3 OS data (24 months away).

branches_required: conditional: file now with Phase 3 running as confirmatory

input: Model: Should we add a biomarker enrichment strategy to our Phase 3 trial (restrict to PD-L1 ≥50%) vs run all-comers? The Phase 2 subgroup showed HR 0.55 in PD-L1 high vs HR 0.88 in PD-L1 low.

branches_required: adaptive enrichment (start all-comers, enrich at interim)
key_factors: enrichment increases effect size but shrinks market
expected_details.recommended_branch: adaptive enrichment with pre-specified decision rules

input: Model scenarios: IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) Medicare drug price negotiation expands to cover all drugs after 5 years on market (currently 9 years for small molecules). How does this affect biotech portfolio strategy?

Model: Phase 2 data shows your CDK4/6 inhibitor has ORR 25% as monotherapy vs ORR 52% in combination with fulvestrant in HR+/HER2- breast cancer. Should you pursue monotherapy, combination, or both in Phase 3?

branches_required: combination with monotherapy as secondary endpoint
key_factors: monotherapy ORR 25% is marginal for approval alone
two Phase 3 trials = 2x cost and complexity
monotherapy label would be valuable but risky
expected_details.recommended_branch: combination Phase 3 with embedded monotherapy cohort

input: Model: Your drug is approved in the US and EU. Should you pursue approval in China (NMPA pathway) vs Japan (PMDA) first? Budget allows one at a time.

key_factors: China: larger patient population, lower pricing, regulatory complexity, IP concerns
Japan: higher pricing, well-characterized pathway, bridging study may suffice
strategic partnering opportunities in each market
